titles.title: Portage: Shared Stewardship of Research Data

descriptions.description: This poster presents Portage, Canada's national research data management (RDM) initiative managed by the Canadian Association of Research Libraries (CARL). Portage provides a library-based network of experts across Canada (97 volunteers in 4 regions, representing 40 organizations) and three shared platform services: (1) DMP Assistant — bilingual online tool for creating Data Management Plans; (2) Federated Research Data Repository (FRDR) — scalable national platform for data deposit, discovery, and preservation; (3) Dataverse North — community of practice for Dataverse repository users. Coverage spans the full research data lifecycle: Plan, Create, Process, Analyze, Disseminate, Preserve, Reuse.

sections.sectionContent: Portage is a national research data management (RDM) initiative to assist researchers and other stakeholders through a library-based network of expertise, and develop national platforms and services for planning, preserving, and discovering research data. Managed by the Canadian Association of Research Libraries.

Practices applied throughout the lifecycle of a research project to guide the collection, documentation, storage, sharing, and preservation of research data. Processes integral to conducting responsible research and helping researchers adhere to institutional and funding agency guidelines.

Areas of Expertise: Data Management Planning, Data Discovery, Curation, Preservation, Training, Research Intelligence, Institutional Planning, RDM Ethics.

Pan-Canadian Representation: Atlantic (10), Quebec (11), Ontario (37), West (39) volunteers. Organizations: CARL University (24), Non-CARL University (7), Other Organization (9).

Online, bilingual data management planning tool (www.assistant.portagenetwork.ca) covering: Data Collection, Documentation and Metadata, Storage and Backup, Preservation, Sharing and Reuse, Responsibilities and Resources, Ethics and Legal Compliance.

A scalable, federated platform for digital RDM and discovery of Canadian research data (www.frdr.ca). Indexes Canadian data repositories from a single platform. Storage and preservation for large datasets. Curation and preservation processes to ensure long-term data usability.
A community of practice for libraries using or interested in using the Dataverse repository platform for research data in Canada.
On-demand training assistance available to those organizing RDM training on Canadian academic campuses. Online training modules in French and English in development.
